source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A gray square with an area of $36 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ and a black square with an area of $25 \mathrm{~cm}^{2}$ are superimposed, as shown beside. What is the perimeter of the overlapping region, represented by the white quadrilateral, which has a vertex on the side of the gray square?
<image1>
extraction_notes: answer letter mapped to option text using option order

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'as shown beside,' indicating the needed geometry comes from the diagram
The target is the perimeter of the overlapping white quadrilateral, whose shape/location depends on the superimposed squares in the image
asset_registry_record shows image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Problem combines given square areas (36 cm^2 and 25 cm^2) with a diagram-based overlap/perimeter request, so it is not a single-step text lookup
Likely requires deriving side lengths from areas and then using the visual configuration of the overlap
The question asks about a specific overlapping region with a vertex on a side of the gray square, suggesting at least two linked geometric inferences
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric result ('$18 \mathrm{~cm}$')
There are multiple-choice options, giving an additional discrete verification target
The geometry task has a well-defined final quantity (perimeter), which is amenable to rule-based or solver-based checking

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产齐全，asset registry passed 且无登记问题
图像依赖度高，但所需示意图文件已存在，因此核心语义可恢复且可用
multi_step_score 和 verifiability_score 都较高，样本质量与可校验性较好
虽然文本中未完整指定几何关系，但该风险已被现有图像覆盖，不构成降级或拒绝的充分理由
